item.title: World leaders want American AI. They just don’t want America to be able to turn it off.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: French President Macron and Indian PM Modi raised alarms at the G7 summit that the U.S. could cut off access to American AI overnight — a fear the Anthropic blackout just made real.

item.title: Anthropic becomes first AI startup to join the Frontier carbon removal coalition
item.dc:creator: Tim De Chant
item.description: Anthropic has joined the Frontier coalition, which received another $915M in pledges to fund carbon removal projects.

item.title: Cybercriminals allegedly hacked tens of thousands of Fortinet firewalls used by major companies all over the world
item.dc:creator: Lorenzo Franceschi-Bicchierai
item.description: An alleged Russian-speaking group of cybercriminals is reportedly compromising and targeting several major companies that use Fortinet Firewalls and VPNs through previously known passwords.

item.title: Social media’s next evolution: User-controlled algorithms
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: Social media feeds are becoming more customizable as platforms like Threads, Instagram, and TikTok introduce tools that let users directly influence the algorithms powering their recommendations.

item.title: World model maker Odyssey nabs $1.45B valuation backed by Amazon and other big names
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: World models are the next big thing in AI beyond LLMs and, with this round, Odyssey has cemented itself as one of the startups to watch.

item.title: Mastodon looks to newsletters to help revive the open social web
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Mastodon’s newly launched newsletter feature lets anyone subscribe to creators by email, even without a Mastodon account.

item.title: Only 16 percent of Americans think AI will have a positive impact on society, a new study shows
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: Although Wall Street loves AI, every day Americans are significantly less optimistic about the industry, a new report from Pew Research shows.

item.title: Two Stanford grads raise $11M to build a noninvasive wearable for hormone tracking
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: Clair Health will track inflammation and bloating markers, energy levels, and cycle phase classification to give insights into cycle irregularities and perimenopause, as well as hormonal fluctuations, and how to navigate those changes.

item.title: Google bets on Gemini to reinvent the smart home speaker
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Google is betting generative AI can breathe new life into the smart speaker. The company's new $99.99 Google Home Speaker replaces the rigid commands of the Google Assistant era with more conversational Gemini interactions.

item.title: PayPal Ventures shutters as company restructuring continues
item.dc:creator: Dominic-Madori Davis
item.description: The corporate venture arm ends after 10 years and 80 investments.

item.title: The slowtech revolution is here to kill your phone addiction and rescue your attention span
item.dc:creator: Amanda Silberling
item.description: “People just really want to take back control of their time, their lives, their attention... They’re down for whatever helps them do that.”

item.title: Collecting robot training data is dirty, unglamorous work. Some AI labs are already paying XDOF to do it.
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: If physical AI is going to match the accomplishments of LLMs, there's a data problem that needs to be solved.

item.title: Pramaana Labs raises $27M seed round from Khosla Ventures to bring formal verification to AI
item.dc:creator: Russell Brandom
item.description: Pramaana will focus on highly sensitive verticals like law, drug discovery, and tax preparation — where errors can be costly and reliability is at a premium.

item.title: SpaceX alum nabs $22M to turn rocket engines into geothermal power plants
item.dc:creator: Tim De Chant
item.description: Critical Energy is turning rocket engines into geothermal power plants, and it wants to build 300 GW per year by 2045.

item.title: Canadian pension giant joins race to fund India’s AI-fueled data center boom
item.dc:creator: Jagmeet Singh
item.description: The Canadian pension giant will acquire an 8.2% stake in CtrlS, a tech giant that operates more than 15 data centers across India.

item.title: DeepL acquires Mixhalo for live-event audio streaming and translation
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: With this acquisition, DeepL is opening an office in San Francisco to expand its U.S. business.

item.title: Uber will bring its premium robotaxi service to Houston in 2027
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Korosec
item.description: This will be the second market to have an Uber robotaxi service outfitted with Lucid EVs equipped with a self-driving system from Nuro.

item.title: Pinterest launches an experimental AI shopping app called ‘Ask Pinterest’
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Pinterest has launched 'Ask Pinterest,' an experimental AI-powered shopping app that lets users seek recommendations and inspiration through a conversational interface.

item.title: Anthropic’s latest feud with the Trump admin may actually help it, sales data suggests
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: Anthropic's popularity with business users is growing so well that the latest beef with the government might actually boost it, data from Ramp suggests.

item.title: Apple plans to change its Hide My Email privacy feature that could make it less effective
item.dc:creator: Zack Whittaker
item.description: In the coming weeks, Apple will move anonymously generated emails addresses to a different domain.
